name: Cataplana de Marisco
description: An Algarvian shellfish stew cooked in a traditional copper cataplana — a fragrant one-pot celebration of the sea.

recipeInstructions.text: Heat olive oil in the cataplana (or a wide heavy-based pot with a lid). Sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add chouriço, red pepper, tomatoes, and paprika. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring.
Pour in wine. Add clams, mussels, and prawns. Season with salt and pepper.
Close the cataplana and cook over medium-high heat for 8–10 minutes, shaking occasionally, until all shellfish have opened.
Discard any shellfish that haven't opened. Garnish with coriander and serve straight from the cataplana with crusty bread.

notes.title: Equipment
notes.text: A traditional copper cataplana gives the best results, but a deep lidded sauté pan works too.
